prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly black frame; no clear objects or people are visible.

Later, it cuts to a close-up product shot of a green bottle. The label is clearly visible and reads “refreshing Pert shampoo,” with large black “Pert” text and orange and blue swoosh-like graphics over a light patterned background.

At the end, the commercial shows three women standing close together and facing the camera. The woman in the center has long blonde hair and wears a light or white top. The woman on the right has lighter gray-blonde hair and also appears to wear a light top. A third woman with dark hair is only partly visible on the left edge of the frame. They appear to be smiling. The background looks like an indoor setting, with a light window or wall area on the left and a darker area behind them. Audio, dialogue, or any slogan is not visible in the frames.
